title: vizdiff SKILL.md references sibling skills under the wrong namespace bento:vizline
description:
The plugin is named `bugshot` (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json:2`), so its skills resolve as `bugshot:vizline` etc. But `skills/vizdiff/SKILL.md` tells the agent to run `bento:vizline` at lines 24, 26, 35, and 108. An agent following these instructions fails to resolve the skill — or worse, resolves into the unrelated `bento` plugin's namespace. Fix: replace all four occurrences with `bugshot:vizline` (or a plain `vizline` with a "this plugin's" qualifier). Audit the other two SKILL.md files for the same mistake.

Acceptance: no `bento:` skill references remain anywhere in bugshot's skills; cross-references resolve to bugshot's own namespace.

title: Persist Bugshot review output artifacts
description: Bugshot currently prints final issue drafts to stdout and removes the temporary SQLite database at session shutdown. Long JSON output can exceed terminal capture limits, which loses comments. Change Bugshot so the final issue draft payload is also written to a temporary file, and preserve the temporary database after completion so agents can recover full comments if stdout capture is truncated.

title: Show review images at native size
description: Bugshot gallery currently appears to force screenshots of different viewport sizes into a consistent display area, making mobile screenshots such as 16-mobile-view-dark.png appear zoomed relative to desktop screenshots. Review images should render at their native pixel size without forced consistency across review units. Acceptance: gallery image CSS/layout preserves each asset's intrinsic dimensions while still allowing viewport scrolling when an image is larger than the browser window.
notes: Final no-ff merge commit: ec074a7f9da17e72b481a6dec200d6108501a5ed landed on main. Earlier close reason referenced transient local merge 44f444750711989065f6fb3e72bd5fab5d202daa before git pull --rebase reconstructed the required no-ff merge.

title: Fix bugshot skill: prohibit background execution, enforce foreground-only
description: The bugshot SKILL.md step 5 says 'even if the CLI is running as a background command', which explicitly permits background execution. Agents exploit this to run the CLI in the background so they can show the URL to the user immediately — but then fail to capture JSON output and can't properly wait for the process. Fix: remove the background-mode escape hatch, add explicit foreground-only guidance to step 4.

title: Selection coordinate offset bug: can't select bottom, can select into header
description:
Selection rectangle behavior is misaligned vertically:

- Cannot draw a selection at the bottom of the screenshot (selection appears clipped or unreachable).
- Can draw a selection past the top of the screenshot, extending up into the page header area.

Symptoms suggest a missing/incorrect Y offset when translating mouse coordinates to image coordinates — likely the header height isn't being subtracted, or scroll offset is wrong, so the entire selectable region is shifted upward by some constant.

Repro: open the gallery, try to draw a selection rectangle near the very bottom edge of an image, then near the top.
close_reason: Aligned region overlay to image offset; previously sat at card origin and missed bottom while extending into title.

title: Add a wire/install skill that creates the vizline/vizdiff trigger files (capture-command, optional baseline)
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

Bugshot ships exactly three skills (`skills/bugshot/`, `skills/vizdiff/`,
`skills/vizline/` in /home/ketan/project/bugshot) and no installer or
wiring skill. Contrast the shatter plugin, which ships `install-shatter`,
`add-shatter-target`, and `wire-shatter-ci` alongside its run skills.

The vizline/vizdiff triggers are file-existence gates:

- vizline triggers when `.agent-plugins/bento/bugshot/viz/capture-command`
  exists in the worktree;
- vizdiff triggers when `.bugshot/baseline/manifest.json` exists.

Nothing in the plugin creates either file, so in a repo that has never
been hand-wired the skills can never fire. Pickpackit case
(/home/ketan/project/pickpackit): the repo has a full Playwright demo
harness producing 36 review screenshots per run (`make demo`,
`web/demo/`) — ideal capture-command material — yet neither
`.agent-plugins/bento/bugshot/viz/capture-command` nor `.bugshot/` exists
(verified absent). Meanwhile a duplicated nav button and clipped labels
sat visible in EVERY screenshot for weeks; vizline/vizdiff never fired
because the trigger files never existed. The plugin's value was zero in
exactly the repo best equipped to feed it.

## Desired behavior / change

Add a wire/install skill (naming up to the repo, e.g. `wire-bugshot`)
that:

- Detects or asks for the project's deterministic screenshot command (in
  pickpackit's case, the existing demo runner).
- Writes `.agent-plugins/bento/bugshot/viz/capture-command` honoring the
  verified contract from the shipped design spec (plugin cache 1.0.17,
  `docs/specs/2026-04-25-vizdiff-vizline-design.md`): invoked as
  `capture-command <output-dir>`, CWD is the worktree the skill operates
  in, and it must produce deterministic relative paths under the output
  dir.
- Optionally seeds an initial `.bugshot/baseline/` (manifest + images)
  from a clean run so vizdiff is armed immediately, with user approval.
- Documents the wiring in the project's agent docs so future sessions
  know the triggers are live.

## Acceptance checks

- Running the new skill in a fixture repo with a trivial screenshot
  command results in an executable
  `.agent-plugins/bento/bugshot/viz/capture-command` that vizline then
  accepts end-to-end (`capture-command <output-dir>` produces images at
  deterministic relative paths).
- The optional baseline-seeding path produces a
  `.bugshot/baseline/manifest.json` that vizdiff accepts.
- The skill refuses or clearly warns when the candidate capture command
  is nondeterministic (e.g. timestamps in filenames), rather than wiring
  a flaky trigger.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: the new skill, its SKILL.md, contract-conformant file
  creation, fixture-level verification.
- Out of scope: changing the vizline/vizdiff trigger contract itself;
  review-completion markers (companion issue bugshot-bs7); wiring
  pickpackit specifically (that is a downstream consumer task).

title: Published plugin bundle is 124 MB; 122 MB is node_modules
description:
The installed cache at `~/.claude/plugins/cache/bento/bugshot/1.0.17/` measures 124 MB, of which `node_modules/` (vitest toolchain and dev dependencies) is 122 MB; the bundle also ships `.beads/`, `docs/plans/`, and the full test suite. Every consumer install drags all of it. Fix: exclude dev dependencies, `.beads/`, `tests/`, and internal plan docs from the published artifact (packaging manifest or build step); if runtime node deps are genuinely needed, ship a production-only install or document a postinstall.

Acceptance: published bundle size reduced by roughly two orders of magnitude; gallery and CLI still function from a fresh install.

Current Code Facts (resolved 2026-06-10): node_modules is NOT tracked in git (git ls-files shows zero node_modules entries), yet the installed cache at 1.0.17 contains 122 MB of it — the first task is determining how it gets into the cache (postinstall? an npm install run inside the cache dir?). Claude plugin installs clone the repo, so repo-tracked content (tests/, docs/plans/, .beads/) ships unavoidably without a packaging step; decide between adding a packaging/publish step, slimming the repo layout, or documented cache hygiene. The repo's package.json (246 bytes) declares no files/bundleDependencies fields.

title: Unify bind-address policy between bugshot and vizdiff gallery servers
description:
Same gallery server, opposite security defaults: the bugshot skill uses the `select-bind-address` helper (binds 127.0.0.1 unless SSH signals indicate remote access; `skills/bugshot/SKILL.md:39-48`), while vizdiff defaults to `0.0.0.0` with a `--local-only` opt-out (`skills/vizdiff/SKILL.md:62-63,77-78`). Fix: have the vizdiff CLI call the same `select-bind-address` helper (or document explicitly why diff review is intentionally network-exposed by default). Note the operator's global instructions tell agents to bind 0.0.0.0 — the helper exists precisely to make a smarter per-environment decision, so the plugin should be internally consistent about using it.

Acceptance: both skills produce the same bind decision in the same environment; the policy is stated once.

Post-review recommendation: unify on the select-bind-address helper for both skills; retain vizdiff's --local-only as an explicit override flag.

title: bugshot SKILL.md step 6 announces a "running" gallery after the blocking CLI call has exited
description:
`skills/bugshot/SKILL.md:72-76`: step 5 establishes that the gallery CLI is a blocking foreground call that returns only after the user finishes reviewing; step 6 then says "Once the tool call returns (after the user has finished reviewing), print the URL ... 'Bugshot gallery is running at `<url>`. Open that URL, review...'" — temporally impossible; the server is down by then. Likely drift from an earlier background-mode design. Fix: reword step 6 as a post-session summary ("The review session at `<url>` has ended; processing N issue drafts") and rely on the streamed stderr for the live URL during the session.

Acceptance: no instruction asks the agent to advertise a live URL after the blocking call returns; the live-URL surface during the session is documented (stderr stream).

Post-review amendment: a repo grep (2026-06-10) found no stderr URL print in the gallery CLI — do not assume one exists. Determine the actual live-URL surface during the blocking call by inspecting the CLI; if none exists, add one (print the URL to stderr at server start) as part of this fix, then document it in step 6's replacement text.

title: vizdiff flag list contradicts itself on feature_worktree; startup step ignores manifest mode
description:
`skills/vizdiff/SKILL.md:69-70` lists "positional `feature_worktree` — required." immediately followed by "positional `feature_worktree` — required unless `--manifest` is supplied." — a leftover from adding manifest mode. Startup step 1 (~line 47 vs 55-59) likewise unconditionally says "Validate `{{feature_worktree}}` is a git worktree" even though manifest mode needs no worktree. Fix: delete the stale line 69; make Startup step 1 conditional on non-manifest mode; verify the prose matches the CLI's actual argparse behavior for both modes.

Acceptance: one consistent statement of when feature_worktree is required; startup steps correct for both modes; prose checked against the CLI parser.

title: Add fullsize image toggle to review-unit detail page
description:
## Current Behavior

On the review-unit detail page, images are constrained to the width of the detail layout and cannot be viewed at their native resolution. Clicking an image does nothing (clicks are consumed by the annotation/region-drawing system). There is no toggle or control to expand to full size.

## Desired Behavior

A fullsize toggle on the detail page analogous to the `s` key shortcut on the index page. When active, the image renders at its native resolution (e.g. 1280×800) rather than being constrained to the panel width, with horizontal scrolling if needed.

## Reproduction

1. Capture screenshots at 1280×800 (e.g. `make demo` in a pickpackit worktree).
2. Open the bugshot gallery on the review-units directory.
3. Click into any review unit to reach the detail page.
4. Observe the image rendered narrower than 1280px with no expand control.
5. Compare: on the index page, `s` toggles fullsize mode (`fullsize-mode` CSS class; `width: auto; max-width: none` on `.fullsize-mode .gallery-item img`). No equivalent exists on the detail page.

## Relevant Source

- `static/style.css` lines 336-390: thumbnail/fullsize mode CSS
- `static/gallery.js` lines 5-6, 149-151: `SHORTCUT_KEY_SIZE = "s"`, `toggleIndexSize()` gated on `isIndex === true` only
- `templates/detail.html`: no fullsize toggle present

## Acceptance Checks

- A toggle (keyboard shortcut, button, or both) on the detail page switches between constrained and native-resolution display.
- Toggle active state is visually indicated.
- Toggling does not interfere with the annotation/region-drawing system.
- When image exceeds viewport width in fullsize mode, container scrolls horizontally rather than clipping.
- Index-page `s` shortcut behavior is unchanged.

## In Scope

- Fullsize toggle UI and/or keyboard shortcut on the detail page.
- CSS to support native-resolution rendering on the detail page.

## Out of Scope

- Changes to index-page fullsize mode.
- Zoom beyond native resolution.
- Changes to the annotation/region-drawing system.

## Open Questions for Implementer

- Preferred interaction surface: button, keyboard shortcut, or both. If a key, confirm no conflict with annotation shortcuts on the detail page.
- Whether fullsize state persists across detail-page navigations within a session.

title: Bump plugin version
description:
Context: The user requested a version bump for the Bugshot plugin.

Desired behavior: Increment the plugin patch version using the repository version/build workflow so generated manifests stay aligned.

Acceptance checks:
- plugin-version.json is bumped by one patch version.
- Generated plugin manifests reflect the bumped version.
- Relevant build command succeeds.

Scope: In scope: version metadata and generated manifest updates. Out of scope: feature or behavior changes.

title: Make vizline default to branch-start target worktree capture
description:
Context: Vizline currently always creates an ephemeral detached base-ref worktree, runs .agent-plugins/bento/bugshot/viz/capture-command there, and copies the result into the target feature worktree. That behavior remains useful when a baseline must be captured after feature work has already begun, but it is unnecessary for the default branch-start workflow.

Desired behavior: Change vizline so the default branch-start path captures the baseline directly in the supplied --feature-worktree. Preserve an explicit opt-in path that uses the ephemeral base-ref worktree for later baseline capture after work has begun.

Acceptance checks:
- Default vizline run uses the target feature worktree capture-command and does not call git worktree add/remove.
- An explicit CLI option still supports capturing from the base ref through an ephemeral worktree for after-work-has-begun scenarios.
- Vizline docs and skill text explain when to use the default versus the ephemeral mode.
- Vizdiff documentation or user-facing guidance notes that if no baseline exists after work has begun, the user should first run vizline in the explicit base-ref/ephemeral mode rather than capturing the already-modified worktree as baseline.
- Existing or updated tests cover the default target-worktree path and the explicit ephemeral path.

Scope: In scope: vizline CLI/workflow behavior, tests, and related docs/skill text including vizdiff notes. Out of scope: gallery UI changes and unrelated Bugshot review flow changes.
notes: Final explicit merge commit: 105cbd4aaed715316ede1cbb56b91d6eb7a7a6ff landed on main with verified tree 610a8180abd6ae53421e43210a23064ff796bece. Earlier close reason references superseded local merge 37e87320 before re-creating the required explicit merge commit.

title: Let Bugshot skill choose session artifact directory
description:
Bugshot currently creates its retained temporary SQLite database and .root sidecar inside the system temp directory from inside gallery_server.create_server. The invoking skill can infer the sidecar from the retained DB path only after the CLI finishes, but it does not know or control the target directory before launch.

Desired behavior:
- Add a CLI option that lets the caller pass an explicit session artifact directory for the temporary database and .root sidecar.
- The gallery server should create the DB and sidecar inside that directory when provided, while preserving current temp-directory behavior when the option is omitted.
- The Bugshot skill should create a session artifact directory before invoking the CLI, print or otherwise keep the path visible to the calling agent, and pass it to the CLI.
- The retained DB and .root sidecar should still share the same stem, and the sidecar should contain the absolute review-root path.

Acceptance checks:
- Unit coverage verifies create_server places the DB and .root sidecar in an explicit session directory.
- CLI/workflow coverage verifies the new option is accepted and passed through.
- Skill documentation shows the agent-created session artifact directory and passes it in the foreground CLI invocation.
- Relevant pytest tests and frontend/build gates pass.

In scope:
- CLI flag and workflow plumbing for the session artifact directory.
- Gallery server support for explicit session artifact directory.
- Bugshot skill invocation docs and generated skill payload sync.

Out of scope:
- Changing the database schema or review unit discovery.
- Deleting intentionally retained workflow artifacts after review completion.

title: Bugshot chat agent — Increment 5: mid-session UI toggle
description:
# Bugshot chat agent — Increment 5: mid-session UI toggle

## Summary

Add a header control in the bugshot gallery that toggles chat on and off
mid-session, without restarting bugshot. Useful when bugshot was launched
without `--chat` and the user discovers during review that they need it.

## Context

After increments 1-4, chat is enabled at launch via `--chat` and stays on
for the whole session. The user has indicated they want a UI toggle so the
gallery itself can enable chat (and so the host agent doesn't need to know
in advance whether the user will want it).

Because the chat agent already spawns lazily (no SDK process exists for a
unit until its first user message), enabling chat mid-session is just a
gating change plus credential detection.

## Scope

**In:**

- New SQLite table (or reuse the existing `session` keyspace with a
  prefix — either is fine):

  ```sql
  CREATE TABLE chat_config (
    key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    value TEXT
  );
  ```

  Used keys: `enabled` (`'true' | 'false'`).
- New endpoint `POST /api/chat/config` accepting `{enabled: bool}`.
  - On `enabled=true`: run the same credential check used at launch. On
    success, persist the new state and return `{ok: true, enabled: true}`.
    On failure, return `{ok: false, error: <reason>}` and leave persisted
    state unchanged.
  - On `enabled=false`: persist new state, return `{ok: true,
    enabled: false}`. In-flight turns complete; new user-message POSTs to
    `/api/chat/{unit_id}` get rejected with a clear error while disabled.
- Header control in `static/gallery.ts`:
  - Compact button or switch labeled "Chat: off" / "Chat: on (N active)"
    (active = units with at least one message in this session).
  - Click toggles via the new endpoint.
  - On failure response, show an inline banner with the error reason
    (e.g., "No Anthropic credentials available — set
    `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or run `claude login`").
  - State hydrated from `chat_config` on page load so a browser reload
    keeps the toggle correct.
- `bugshot_cli.py` change: when bugshot is launched without `--chat`, the
  workflow still wires up the chat agent module so that toggling on
  works. The SDK is still only spawned lazily on first message; nothing
  is paid for if the toggle is never flipped.

**Out (follow-ups):**

- Per-unit toggling (chat enabled on some units, disabled on others).
  Global toggle only for this increment.

## Acceptance checks

- New unit tests in `tests/test_chat_endpoints.py` (extended) cover:
  - toggle on with creds available: state persists, `enabled=true`
    returned
  - toggle on with creds missing: state unchanged, error returned
  - toggle off: state persists, subsequent `POST /api/chat/{unit_id}`
    rejected
  - state persists across server restart within the same DB
- Frontend tests cover toggle button states and banner display on
  cred-failure response.
- New `tests/e2e_chat_toggle.sh`: launch bugshot WITHOUT `--chat`, toggle
  chat on via the API, send a message, verify chat works. Then toggle off
  and verify new messages are rejected.
- Existing tests still pass, including the existing e2e (which launches
  without `--chat`; the toggle defaulting to off must preserve current
  behavior).
- Manual smoke: launch bugshot against a fixture without `--chat`, click
  the toggle in the gallery header, observe credential check, send a
  message.
- Manual smoke (failure path): unset `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and
  `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, launch bugshot, click toggle, observe error
  banner; chat stays disabled.

## Reproduction / evidence

Feature add on top of increments 1-4. Before: chat requires `--chat` at
launch. After: chat can be enabled from the gallery UI mid-session.

## Open items for implementer

- Visual placement of the toggle in the existing gallery header — should
  fit alongside the existing "Done Reviewing" button.
- Whether the credential-failure error banner is dismissible or sticky
  until the user fixes their environment.

## Rough size

Small-medium. New table or key, one endpoint, one UI control plus banner,
small wiring change in `bugshot_cli.py`.

## Dependency

Blocked by increment 1. Independent of increments 2, 3, 4 (but the
"`N active`" counter is most meaningful once increments 2/3 give chat
real value).

## Tracker policy

Normal `ready` issue once a fresh-reviewer precheck passes (logically
blocked-by increment 1). Project tracker: Beads (`bd`), prefix `bugshot`.

title: Bugshot chat agent — Increment 4: transcript persistence and contract
description:
# Bugshot chat agent — Increment 4: transcript persistence and output contract

## Summary

Persist each unit's chat transcript to a temporary directory and surface
that directory's path back to the calling agent via the bugshot stdout
JSON. Each draft whose unit had a chat also gains a `transcript_path`
field. This is the contract by which a calling Claude session learns what
investigations happened during a bugshot review, so it can read them
before filing tracker issues.

## Context

After increments 1-3, chat works end-to-end and findings can become real
comments. But on bugshot exit, the only thing the calling agent receives
is the existing draft JSON — no investigation context. If the agent files
a tracker issue from a draft that came from a chat, it has no way to
include the chat's reasoning.

Per the user's instruction, transcripts are written to a temp directory
and shared by path (not embedded in the JSON), keeping stdout payloads
small.

## Scope

**In:**

- On session start, when chat is enabled, create
  `/tmp/bugshot_transcripts_<random>/` via
  `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="bugshot_transcripts_")`.
- For every unit that has at least one row in `chat_messages`, write
  `<unit_id>.md` to the transcript directory. Format:

  ```markdown
  # Investigation: <unit_id>
  *<unit label>* — chat opened <ISO-8601 timestamp>

  ## Context
  - Assets: <comma-separated asset filenames>
  - Metadata: <comma-separated metadata filenames>
  - Existing comments: <count>

  ## Conversation

  ### user (HH:MM:SS)
  <body>

  ### assistant (HH:MM:SS)
  *tool: <tool_name> <short args> — <short result summary>*
  *tool: ...*

  <assistant text>

  **Suggested comment** (accepted | dismissed | pending):
  > <body>
  ```

- Write `index.json` to the same directory:

  ```json
  {
    "<unit_id>": {
      "transcript_path": "<absolute path>",
      "message_count": <int>,
      "suggestion_count": <int>,
      "accepted_count": <int>
    },
    ...
  }
  ```

- Extend `ReviewSummary` (`bugshot_workflow.py:69-72`):

  ```python
  @dataclass
  class ReviewSummary:
      draft_count: int
      drafts: list[dict[str, object]]
      transcript_dir: str | None = None
      investigation_count: int = 0     # units with chat but no resulting draft
  ```

- Extend the draft-building path (`_process_comments` around
  `bugshot_workflow.py:268-290`): for each draft, look up whether its
  `unit_id` had any `chat_messages` rows; if so, attach
  `transcript_path: <absolute path>` to that draft.
- Stderr message: `Bugshot transcripts written to <dir>` (mirrors the
  existing `Bugshot issue draft JSON written to ...` convention around
  `bugshot_workflow.py:114-117`).
- Stdout JSON (when `--json`) gains `transcript_dir` and
  `investigation_count` fields. `investigation_count` = units with chat
  activity but no resulting accepted suggestion (i.e., no draft they
  contribute to).
- Update documentation:
  - `skills/bugshot/SKILL.md`: document the new fields and the agent
    contract ("When `transcript_path` is set on a draft, read the
    transcript before filing the tracker issue and incorporate the
    investigation reasoning").
  - `skills/bugshot/overlays/claude.md` and `codex.md` updated
    consistently.
  - Rebuild generated skill payloads via `scripts/build-plugin` (per
    `AGENTS.md` "Documentation Sync Rules").

**Out (follow-ups):**

- Unifying drafts JSON, DB, and transcripts under a single per-run parent
  directory (commit 31a06d4 left drafts JSON and DB as loose tempfiles).
  Track as a separate cleanup issue after this increment lands.

## Acceptance checks

- New `tests/test_transcript_emission.py` covers:
  - transcript directory created when chat is enabled and at least one
    message exists
  - one markdown file per chatted unit, with the documented sections
  - `index.json` contents match expected counts
  - `ReviewSummary` populates `transcript_dir` and `investigation_count`
  - drafts whose unit had chat receive `transcript_path`
  - drafts whose unit had no chat do NOT receive `transcript_path`
  - chat disabled → no transcript directory created, no `transcript_dir`
    field in stdout JSON
- New `tests/e2e_chat_transcripts.sh`: end-to-end with chat + suggestion
  + accept; final stdout JSON contains `transcript_dir`; a draft with
  `transcript_path`; the file on disk contains the expected conversation
  including tool-call lines and the accepted-suggestion marker.
- Existing tests still pass, including the existing e2e flow with chat
  disabled (no transcript-related fields appear).
- Manual smoke: launch bugshot from a Claude Code session via the skill
  with `--chat`; verify the calling agent receives JSON with
  `transcript_dir`, can read a transcript markdown, and uses it when
  filing a tracker issue.

## Reproduction / evidence

Feature add on top of increments 1-3. Before: bugshot stdout JSON has
only `draft_count` + `drafts`. After: it also has `transcript_dir` and
`investigation_count`; draft objects optionally have `transcript_path`.

## Open items for implementer

- Markdown rendering details for tool calls — pick truncation thresholds
  and a one-line summary format (e.g.
  `*tool: Grep "auth" components/ — 7 matches*`).
- Whether to record the bugshot version / git SHA in the transcript
  header for forensics. Probably yes; cheap to add.

## Rough size

Medium. Writer + summary plumbing + contract update in SKILL.md + overlay
rebuild + tests. No new UI.

## Dependency

Blocked by increment 1 (uses `chat_messages` table) and increment 3
(transcripts record accepted/dismissed/pending suggestion outcomes from
`chat_suggestions`). Independent of increment 2.

## Tracker policy

Normal `ready` issue once a fresh-reviewer precheck passes (logically
blocked-by increments 1 and 3). Project tracker: Beads (`bd`), prefix
`bugshot`.

title: Bugshot chat agent — Increment 3: propose_comment and suggestion cards
description:
# Bugshot chat agent — Increment 3: `propose_comment` tool and suggestion cards

## Summary

Let the bugshot chat agent (from increments 1 & 2) turn findings into real
comments without the user copy-pasting. The agent calls a new
`propose_comment(body, region?)` tool; the gallery renders the proposal as
an inline card inside the chat thread with Add / Edit&Add / Dismiss
buttons. Accepted suggestions create regular comment rows that flow into
the existing draft pipeline on bugshot exit.

## Context

After increments 1 & 2 the agent can investigate, but findings have to be
manually copy-pasted into a comment by the user. This is the user-visible
payoff of the chat work: investigations cleanly flow into the existing
draft → tracker-issue pipeline.

Design decision (already settled with the user): the suggestion appears
*inline in the chat thread*, not in the comments list and not in a
separate "suggestions" panel. Accepted suggestions land in the existing
`comments` table; until accepted, they remain in `chat_suggestions` with
`status='pending'`.

## Scope

**In:**

- New `propose_comment(body: str, region?: dict)` tool wired into
  `chat_agent.py`.
  - `region` shape matches the existing comment region payload
    (`{type, x, y, w, h, selection_id?}`, normalized to [0,1]).
- New SQLite table:

  ```sql
  CREATE TABLE chat_suggestions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    unit_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    message_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES chat_messages(id),
    body TEXT NOT NULL,
    region TEXT,                       -- JSON, optional
    status TEXT NOT NULL,              -- 'pending' | 'accepted' | 'dismissed'
    accepted_comment_id INTEGER REFERENCES comments(id),
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );
  ```

- New SSE event type emitted by the gallery server when the tool is
  called: `suggestion` with payload
  `{suggestion_id, body, region?, message_id}`.
- New endpoints in `gallery_server.py`:
  - `POST /api/chat/{unit_id}/accept` — body
    `{suggestion_id, body?, region?}` (the `body` / `region` overrides let
    the UI submit edits). Inserts a row in `comments`, sets
    `chat_suggestions.status='accepted'` and `accepted_comment_id`,
    returns the new comment.
  - `POST /api/chat/{unit_id}/dismiss` — body `{suggestion_id}`. Sets
    `chat_suggestions.status='dismissed'`.
- UI (`static/gallery.ts`):
  - Suggestion card component rendered inline in the chat stream when the
    `suggestion` event arrives. Shows body, region preview (reuses
    existing region rendering on the canvas), three buttons.
  - "Edit & add" expands the card into an inline editor pre-filled with
    the suggested body and region; on confirm, calls
    `POST /api/chat/{unit_id}/accept` with overrides.
  - On accept: refresh the comments pane to show the new comment; collapse
    the suggestion card to a compact "Added as comment" line that links
    to the comment.
  - On dismiss: collapse the card to "Dismissed" and stay in the
    transcript.
  - "From chat" badge added to comments whose `source` is the new chat
    flow (small visual differentiation in the comments pane).
- Comment rows from accepted suggestions: leverage the existing `comments`
  schema; add a `source` column (`'user' | 'chat'`) if not already
  present, or store the linkage purely via
  `chat_suggestions.accepted_comment_id` and read it back to set the
  badge.

**Out (later increments):**

- Adding `transcript_path` to draft JSON output — increment 4. Until then,
  drafts created from chat suggestions are indistinguishable from manual
  drafts in the final JSON.

## Acceptance checks

- New unit tests in `tests/test_chat_endpoints.py` cover:
  - `POST .../accept` inserts a row in `comments`, marks suggestion
    accepted, returns the new comment
  - accept with `body` / `region` overrides uses the overrides
  - `POST .../dismiss` marks suggestion dismissed; no `comments` row
    created
  - SSE `suggestion` event fires when the tool is called
- Frontend tests cover:
  - suggestion card renders with body + region preview
  - Add button fires correct POST with no overrides
  - Edit&Add opens editor, Confirm fires POST with overrides
  - Dismiss fires correct POST and collapses the card
- New `tests/e2e_chat_suggestion.sh`: fake SDK calls
  `propose_comment`; UI accepts; final bugshot exit JSON includes the
  resulting draft alongside any drafts from manual comments.
- Manual smoke: against a fixture with a real Claude, ask the agent to
  identify a bug and call `propose_comment`. Verify the card appears,
  accept it, see the comment in the comments pane with a "from chat"
  badge, exit bugshot, observe the draft in the JSON output.
- Existing tests still pass.

## Reproduction / evidence

Feature add. Before: agent has no way to create comments. After: agent
calls `propose_comment`, user accepts (with optional edits), comment
appears in the comments list and becomes a draft on exit.

## Open items for implementer

- Whether to add a `source` column to `comments` or rely solely on
  `chat_suggestions.accepted_comment_id` for the badge join. Either is
  fine; pick whichever is simpler at write time.
- Visual spec for "from chat" badge: small icon + text, fits the existing
  comments-pane aesthetic.

## Rough size

Medium. One new tool, one new endpoint pair, one new table, one new UI
component plus an edit-in-place editor.

## Dependency

Blocked by increment 1 (uses `chat_agent.py` and the chat pane);
independent of increment 2 (does not depend on `Bash`).

## Tracker policy

Normal `ready` issue once a fresh-reviewer precheck passes (logically
blocked-by increment 1). Project tracker: Beads (`bd`), prefix `bugshot`.

title: Bugshot chat agent — Increment 2: Bash tool with strict allowlist
description:
# Bugshot chat agent — Increment 2: Bash tool with strict allowlist

## Summary

Add a `Bash` tool to the bugshot chat agent (introduced in increment 1) so
the agent can run read-only shell commands when investigating a unit
(`git log`, `git blame`, `rg`, etc.). Commands are restricted by a strict
argv-level allowlist; paths are validated to stay under `--repo-root`.

## Context

Increment 1 gives the chat agent `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`. In practice,
investigation often needs `git log`, `git blame`, `git show`, `git diff`,
`find`, and a few other read-only shell utilities. Without `Bash`, the
agent can't trace when something changed or who changed it.

Risk: Bash is general-purpose; a careless implementation lets the agent
mutate the repo, exfiltrate data, or escape `--repo-root`. We mitigate by
allowlisting argv[0], rejecting shell metacharacters, and resolving every
path argument under `--repo-root` before execution.

## Scope

**In:**

- New `Bash` tool wired into `chat_agent.py` (or its tool module).
- Argv parsing: shell-style tokenization via `shlex.split`; reject input
  containing `|`, `>`, `<`, `&&`, `||`, `;`, `` ` ``, `$(`, backticks
  (the agent should issue follow-up tool calls instead).
- Strict allowlist (case-sensitive, first-token only):

  ```
  cat, head, tail, less, ls, wc, file, stat,
  grep, rg, find, fd, sort, uniq, awk, sed,
  git log, git diff, git show, git blame, git status, git ls-files,
  jq, yq, tree, basename, dirname, pwd, echo
  ```

  - For `git` subcommands: the first two tokens (`git <subcommand>`) must
    match; reject `git push`, `git commit`, etc.
  - For `sed`: reject `-i` (and `--in-place`) explicitly so it can't
    mutate files.
- Path-argument validation: any token that exists or resolves under the
  filesystem must resolve to a path under `--repo-root` after symlink
  resolution. Tokens that don't look like paths (e.g., grep patterns)
  pass through.
- Working directory for execution: `--repo-root` (not CWD of the bugshot
  process).
- Output: truncated at a sane byte limit (e.g., 64 KiB); the tool result
  records both the truncated stdout and a `truncated: true` flag the agent
  sees.
- UI rendering: the tool-row component from increment 1 already accepts
  arbitrary `tool_name`; verify `Bash` rows render with the command + a
  short result preview, expandable for the full output.

**Out (follow-ups):**

- Loosening the allowlist based on real-world use — leave the lists as
  written and revisit in a follow-up issue once we have usage data.
- Sandboxed execution beyond allowlist + path validation (no chroot, no
  seccomp, no process limits in this increment).

## Acceptance checks

- `tests/test_chat_agent.py` (extended) covers:
  - allowed command success (e.g., `git log --oneline -5`)
  - disallowed command rejection (`rm -rf /`, `git push`,
    `curl example.com`)
  - path-escape rejection: `cat /etc/passwd` returns an error,
    `cat ../outside/file` is rejected when `--repo-root` is a subdirectory
  - shell-metacharacter rejection (`ls | grep foo`, `ls > out.txt`,
    `ls && rm x`, `ls; rm x`, `$(ls)`)
  - `sed -i` rejection
  - truncation: stdout above the byte limit gets a `truncated: true`
    marker
- `python -m pytest tests/ -v` and `npm run build:frontend &&
  npm run test:frontend` pass.
- Existing e2e tests (including increment 1's chat e2e) still pass.
- Manual smoke: ask the agent "what commit last touched
  components/auth/PasswordField.tsx" — the agent issues a `git log`,
  result is captured and displayed.

## Reproduction / evidence

Feature add on top of increment 1. Before: agent can `Read`/`Grep`/`Glob`
but not `git blame` or `git log`. After: agent can issue any command on
the allowlist.

## Open items for implementer

- Final form of the path-detection heuristic for argv tokens. A conservative
  rule: any token starting with `/`, `./`, `../`, or containing `/` is
  treated as a path and validated; bare tokens are passed through.
- Whether to allow long-running commands (`find` over large repos) and how
  to bound runtime. First cut: no timeout; document as a follow-up if it
  becomes a problem.

## Rough size

Smallest of the five increments. Single module change plus tests.

## Dependency

Blocked by increment 1 (this issue extends `chat_agent.py` and the
tool-row UI it introduces).

## Tracker policy

Normal `ready` issue once a fresh-reviewer precheck passes (but logically
blocked-by increment 1). Project tracker: Beads (`bd`), prefix `bugshot`.

title: Bugshot chat agent — Increment 1: bare investigative chat
description:
# Bugshot chat agent — Increment 1: bare investigative chat

## Summary

Add an investigative chat side pane to the bugshot gallery's detail view. A
Claude spawned via the Claude Agent SDK can read the repository (Read,
Grep, Glob) and answer questions about a unit (e.g., "is this a real bug,
and where in the code does it come from?"). End-to-end plumbing only —
findings cannot yet flow into comments/drafts (that's increment 3), and
transcripts are not persisted to disk (increment 4).

## Context

Bugshot today (see `AGENTS.md` and `bugshot_workflow.py`) is one-shot: the
calling agent launches `bugshot_cli.py`, the user clicks through the
gallery in the browser, and the CLI emits issue drafts on exit. The user
reviewing a unit currently has no in-gallery way to investigate whether a
visual oddity is a real bug or where it comes from — they have to leave the
gallery and dig manually.

This increment introduces the smallest end-to-end shape of an investigative
chat: side pane, agent spawn, streaming protocol, three read tools. It
proves the architecture and ships value (the user can ask questions and
copy-paste answers into a manual comment) without depending on later
increments.

The host Claude session that launched bugshot is blocked inside a Bash tool
call and cannot be the chat partner. The chat partner is always a freshly
spawned Claude (Agent SDK), whether bugshot was launched from an agent or
by a human directly.

## Scope

**In:**

- New module `chat_agent.py` with one Agent SDK conversation per
  `unit_id`, spawned lazily on the first user message for that unit.
- Tools available to the agent: `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob` (paths constrained
  to `--repo-root`).
- Gallery server endpoints (`gallery_server.py`):
  - `POST /api/chat/{unit_id}` — submit a user message
  - `GET /api/chat/{unit_id}/stream` — SSE stream of assistant tokens +
    tool call events
  - `GET /api/chat/{unit_id}` — chat history for hydration on page load
- New SQLite table:

  ```sql
  CREATE TABLE chat_messages (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    unit_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    role TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'user' | 'assistant' | 'tool'
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    tool_name TEXT,
    tool_args TEXT,               -- JSON
    tool_result TEXT,             -- truncated
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );
  ```

- New CLI flags on `bugshot_cli.py`:
  - `--chat` (default off; enables chat agent pool)
  - `--repo-root <path>` (defaults to bugshot's CWD; constrains tool paths)
  - `--api-key <key>` (explicit override)
  - `--context-file <path>` (optional priming brief, included in the
    initial system or user message)
- Credential detection order:
  1. `--api-key` flag
  2. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var
  3. `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` env var (reuses interactive Claude Code
     login)
  4. SDK auto-detect (subscription via `claude` binary)
  - Fail at startup with a clear error if `--chat` is set and none
    resolves.
- Detail view (`static/gallery.ts`, `templates/detail.html`):
  - Right-side chat pane, hidden when `--chat` is off.
  - EventSource consumer for the SSE stream.
  - Renders user / assistant messages and tool-call activity as collapsed,
    expandable rows ("Read components/auth/PasswordField.tsx").
- Agent priming on first message: unit's images as vision inputs, contents
  of metadata JSON files (truncated to a reasonable limit), the unit's
  existing user comments, and the `--context-file` if provided.

**Out (later increments):**

- `Bash` tool with strict allowlist — increment 2
- `propose_comment` tool and suggestion-card UI flow that lets chat
  findings become real comments — increment 3
- Transcript persistence to disk and `transcript_dir` / `transcript_path`
  output contract — increment 4
- Mid-session UI toggle — increment 5

## Acceptance checks

- `python -m pytest tests/ -v` passes including new
  `tests/test_chat_agent.py` and `tests/test_chat_endpoints.py`.
- `npm run build:frontend && npm run test:frontend` passes.
- `bash tests/e2e_test.sh` (the existing e2e) still passes — no
  regression in non-chat flows.
- New `tests/e2e_chat_basic.sh` (using a fake Agent SDK that returns
  scripted responses) demonstrates:
  - launching bugshot with `--chat` against a fixture directory,
  - submitting a user message via the chat pane,
  - SSE events arrive (`assistant_token`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`,
    `assistant_done`),
  - `chat_messages` rows persisted.
- Manual smoke (documented in the PR description) with a real
  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`: open a fixture unit, ask a question, agent reads a
  repo file and answers.
- Manual smoke with only `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` set (no
  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`): same flow works.
- With `--chat` not set: gallery behaves identically to today; no chat
  pane is rendered; no SDK process is spawned; no new tables touched
  beyond their `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` init.

## Reproduction / evidence

This is a feature add, not a bug. The user-visible "before" state:
launching bugshot against `tests/fixtures/` shows the gallery exactly as
today. The user-visible "after" state with `--chat --repo-root <repo>` set:
the detail view shows a chat pane on the right; typing a question streams a
reply; tool-call activity appears inline.

## Open items for implementer

- Verify exact Claude Agent SDK auth modes against the pinned version
  before locking the credential detection order; adjust order if any of
  the three primary modes are unsupported.
- Decide message-truncation policy for large metadata JSON files included
  in agent priming.
- Decide what counts as "large" for tool results (truncation threshold
  before they are summarized in the UI / stored as `tool_result`).

## Rough size

Largest of the five increments. New Python module, new endpoints, new SQL
table, new UI pane with streaming, new CLI flags, new tests. Likely 1-2
PRs depending on how the frontend pane is sliced.

## Tracker policy

This is a normal `ready` issue once a fresh-reviewer precheck passes.
Project tracker: Beads (`bd`), prefix `bugshot`.

title: Invoke Bugshot CLI script directly in skill guidance
description:
Bugshot skill guidance currently tells agents not to prefix the gallery invocation with rtk, but still shows python3 {{bugshot_dir}}/bugshot_cli.py. For Codex agent permissions, the executable script itself should be invoked directly so permissions can scope to that script path.

Scope: update the canonical Bugshot skill guidance, Codex overlay wording, generated plugin skill payloads, and focused tests so the gallery process command uses {{bugshot_dir}}/bugshot_cli.py directly, without python3 and without rtk.

Acceptance checks:
- The displayed gallery invocation is {{bugshot_dir}}/bugshot_cli.py --json --bind "" {{directory}}.
- Guidance explicitly says not to use python3 or rtk for this gallery process invocation.
- The script is executable or tests otherwise verify direct invocation remains valid.
- Generated Claude and Codex skill payloads are refreshed.

title: Add review-root sidecar for temporary database
description:
Bugshot currently encodes the review-root basename and a short absolute-path hash into the temporary SQLite database filename. In practice the basename is often generic, such as screenshots or a temporary directory, and the hash is not useful for humans.

Desired behavior:
- The temporary database filename should keep session identity details such as local creation date/time, agent, project, and a uniqueness suffix.
- The temporary database filename should stop including the review-root basename and path hash.
- When the temporary database is created, Bugshot should create a sibling sidecar file with the exact same stem as the database file and a different extension.
- The sidecar file should contain the absolute review-root directory path, as plain text, so a retained or stale DB can be traced back to its source directory.
- Normal cleanup should remove both the temporary database and sidecar file together.
- If the workflow intentionally retains the database path for debugging, the sidecar should remain available alongside it.

Acceptance checks:
- Unit coverage verifies the DB filename no longer includes the review-root basename/path hash.
- Unit coverage verifies the sidecar path shares the DB stem, uses a different extension, and contains the absolute review-root path.
- Unit coverage or existing fixture cleanup verifies normal server cleanup removes the sidecar with the DB.
- Relevant pytest tests pass.

In scope:
- Temporary session database filename generation.
- Sidecar file creation and cleanup for the gallery server/session database.
- Regenerating vendored skill payload copies if source files are copied by the plugin build.

Out of scope:
- Persisting the database or sidecar outside the system temporary directory.
- Changing the SQLite schema or review unit discovery.

title: Improve Bugshot temporary database filenames
description:
Bugshot currently creates its temporary SQLite session database with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile using a path like /tmp/bugshot_q7rdto3g.db. That random-only name is hard to interpret when debugging active or stale sessions.

Desired behavior:
- The temporary database filename should include the local date and time the session was created.
- The filename should include an agent identifier when one can be inferred from the environment, with a sensible fallback.
- The filename should include the project name.
- The filename should include a stable short hint tying it back to the screenshot/review root directory, such as the directory basename plus a short digest of its absolute path.
- The database should still be created in the system temporary directory and should remain safe for arbitrary project or directory names.

Acceptance checks:
- Unit coverage verifies the filename contains sanitized date/time, agent, project, review-root hint, and a uniqueness suffix.
- Existing workflow behavior continues to create and clean up the temporary SQLite database.
- Relevant pytest tests pass.

In scope:
- Filename generation for the Bugshot temporary session database.
- Small helper extraction if needed for testability.

Out of scope:
- Persisting the database outside the temp directory.
- Changing the SQLite schema or gallery session lifecycle.

title: Ensure Bugshot gallery invocation is not prefixed with rtk
description:
Bugshot can print or provide a command used to invoke the gallery process. In this repo, AGENTS.md asks shell commands to be prefixed with rtk, but that prefix must not be applied to the gallery process invocation because it would change what downstream users or tools execute.

Scope: find the code path that constructs, prints, or executes the gallery process invocation and ensure the invocation itself is never prefixed with rtk. Preserve normal agent shell-command guidance elsewhere.

Acceptance checks:
- The gallery process invocation is built without an rtk prefix.
- Automated tests cover the behavior or the closest focused verification is documented.
- Existing Bugshot gallery behavior remains otherwise unchanged.

title: Cache and prefetch gallery images
description: Bugshot should tell browsers to cache served review images for a long time, prefetch all image assets from the index page, and prefetch adjacent previous/next image assets from each individual image page.
notes: Final landing evidence: 7d399cdbb4a3e1e77bfcd00fed4e10c625c8bc66 landed on main with verified tree c72236b32c9da6397676db8a3e8467711c172318.

title: Bump plugin version
description: Bump Bugshot plugin version from 1.0.13 to the next patch version and regenerate plugin manifests using the repo build script. Acceptance: plugin-version.json, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, and .codex-plugin/plugin.json all report the same bumped version.

title: Bump plugin version for skill foreground + source-code-examination changes
Tier 1: vizline/vizdiff hardening — terse skill triggers, SHA verification, .bugshot path guard
description:
Address gaps surfaced in /home/ketan/tmp/2026-05-06-bugshot-vizline-vizdiff-recommendations.md as a single batch of in-repo changes. The trigger problem itself is being solved separately via bento's per-project hook framework (already in launch-work/land-work). This issue covers the bugshot-side guardrails and skill-description improvements so non-bento agents can also fire the skills correctly and silent-corruption modes hard-fail.

Changes:

1. **Terse trigger-only skill descriptions** for vizline, vizdiff, bugshot. Frontmatter is loaded into every agent session at startup; keep it minimal and predicate-driven. Move elaborate prose into the existing 'When to use' / 'Prerequisites' body sections.

2. **Body additions** in vizline/SKILL.md and vizdiff/SKILL.md to absorb the SKIP conditions and lifecycle prose that doesn't belong in the description.

3. **SHA verification on baseline load** — extend baseline_manifest.read_manifest (or add a verify_images helper) to confirm each on-disk image's SHA-256 matches the manifest entry. Call it from vizdiff_workflow._resolve_baseline_source. Catches the silent-corruption mode where someone overwrites .bugshot/baseline/images/ without going through vizline.

4. **Hard-refuse bugshot on .bugshot/-internal paths** — when bugshot is invoked on a directory inside .bugshot/ (baseline/, baseline/images/, head/, etc.), exit non-zero with a message pointing to vizdiff. Catches the actual failure pattern from the recommendations doc (lead invoked plain bugshot on .bugshot/baseline/images/).
design: Frontmatter description format follows the claude-api skill's TRIGGER/SKIP pattern compressed to one or two sentences. Path-guard uses simple Path.parts membership check rather than parent-walk so it catches both .bugshot/baseline/ and .bugshot/baseline/images/ uniformly. SHA-verification is computed lazily — only at vizdiff baseline-load, not at gallery-server startup or per-request — to avoid latency on the gallery hot path.
acceptance_criteria:
- vizline/vizdiff/bugshot SKILL.md frontmatter descriptions are <300 chars each, trigger-predicate driven
- SKILL.md bodies for vizline and vizdiff have explicit SKIP-condition guidance in 'When to use'
- baseline_manifest verifies each image's on-disk SHA-256 against the manifest; tampered baseline → VizdiffError with refresh guidance
- bugshot_cli refuses any path that resolves inside a .bugshot/ directory; error message names vizdiff as the right tool
- All existing tests pass; new tests cover SHA-verification mismatch and .bugshot-path refusal
close_reason: Landed in merge to main; tier-1 hardening complete.

title: Fix Bugshot review unit image order
description: Review units should display the reference image first, the conversion second, then diagnostic images. A recent ordering change made this unstable for Bugshot review units.
notes: Landed on main as merge commit 7a91bdb02c872e48bea05d09e1282866e2249d6c. Rebased feature commit 7242f18dc1d184fc7c22b54746af96ab90add01a. Verified exact merge preview tree 32fb0117e8250357c25117f44beb1c368bb17425 with python3 -m pytest tests/ -q (154 passed) and npm run test:frontend (5 passed).

title: Bump Bugshot plugin version
description: Increment the Bugshot plugin patch version and regenerate versioned plugin metadata.
notes: Bumped plugin version from 1.0.8 to 1.0.9 on branch bugshot/version-bump-1-0-9. Verification: scripts/build-plugin --bump completed, npm run build:frontend passed, pytest tests/test_build_plugin.py tests/test_install_codex_plugin.py -q passed (16 tests).

title: Generalize Bugshot skill packaging for agent-specific overlays
description: Make the canonical Bugshot skill language agent-neutral, then add separate Codex and Claude overlays so Codex can get process-completion guidance without changing Claude behavior. Omit tracker-specific Beads instructions from the reusable plugin guidance.
notes: Implemented on branch bugshot/codex-claude-skill-overlays. Verification passed: pytest tests/ -q (150 passed), npm run build:frontend, npm run test:frontend (5 passed), bash tests/e2e_test.sh (10 passed), and scripts/install-codex-plugin --skip-build --skip-register --dry-run.

title: Separate theme controls from nav row
description: Theme color buttons currently look like the index navigation button and share the same row. Update the gallery UI so theme buttons have a distinct visual treatment and are separated from navigation controls.

title: Float feedback composer above review content
description: Move the new feedback text input into a floating element above the review unit content so reviewers can see the input and images at the same time.
notes: Implementation pushed on branch bugshot/bugshot-1i8-float-feedback-composer. Verification passed: python pytest suite (141 passed) and tests/e2e_test.sh (10 passed). Leave open until branch is landed on main.

title: Add Codex installation docs and script
description: Add INSTALL.md and an installer script that installs the Bugshot Codex plugin from a local checkout. The script should be executable, support help and verbose flags, and document the Codex plugin installation path.
notes: Branch bugshot/bugshot-eqm-codex-install adds INSTALL.md, scripts/install-codex-plugin, and installer tests. Verification: pytest tests/ -q (139 passed), bash tests/e2e_test.sh (10 passed), py_compile for installer, and temp CODEX_HOME registration smoke test.

title: Add automated JavaScript test infrastructure for gallery frontend
description:
The frontend (static/gallery.js, ~2300 lines) has no automated tests. All verification is currently manual or via the Python server-side e2e tests, which only cover the API surface — not selection coordinate math, comment-row interactions, region replace flow, in-place edit lifecycle, keyboard shortcuts, etc.

Pick a lightweight stack (e.g., Vitest + jsdom or Playwright for browser-level tests) and wire it into the test command set. At minimum: cover the region-drawing coordinate translation, commitDrawState (including replace mode), the inline comment editor, and the Esc/keyboard handling.

This was filed after a multi-issue UI pass (slp/zdb/abu/2rm/wvx) where the lack of frontend tests forced reliance on visual inspection.

title: Display screenshots at actual size, don't scale
description:
Screenshots in the gallery are being scaled (likely fit-to-width or similar). They should be displayed at their actual pixel size (1:1) so users see exactly what was captured. Scaling distorts perceived bug severity and makes precise selections harder.

If a screenshot is wider than the viewport, allow horizontal scrolling rather than shrinking it.
close_reason: Render asset-card images at natural size; card scrolls when image exceeds its box. SVG and vizdiff selectors untouched.

title: Edit comments in-place instead of using browser prompt() modal
description:
Editing an existing comment currently uses a JavaScript browser modal (likely window.prompt). Replace this with in-place editing: the comment text becomes an editable textarea inline, with save/cancel affordances.

Browser prompt() modals are clunky — single-line, no formatting, no multi-line support, blocks the page, and looks unpolished.
close_reason: Comment editing now uses an inline textarea + save/cancel + Esc/Ctrl+Enter; prompt() removed.

title: Allow deleting/modifying selections (with and without attached feedback)
description:
Users need a way to manage selections in the bugshot gallery:

1. Delete a selection that has no attached feedback (currently no way to remove a stray selection).
2. Delete or modify a selection that is already attached to feedback.

Design for good usability — consider:
- Discoverability of delete/modify affordances (hover controls? click selection to reveal handles?)
- Modify could mean resizing/moving the selection rectangle, not just deleting and redrawing.
- For selections attached to feedback: deleting the selection shouldn't necessarily delete the comment — clarify the relationship (detach vs. delete both).
- Keyboard support (e.g., Delete/Backspace when a selection is focused).
- Visual feedback for which selection is currently active/selected for editing.
close_reason: Per-comment 'remove region' and 'modify region' (replace-in-place) added; pending-region cancel button covers the unattached-pending case.

title: When user makes a selection, focus the feedback text box
description: After making a selection in the bugshot gallery, focus should automatically move to the feedback text box so the user can start typing their comment immediately without an extra click.
acceptance_criteria:
- After completing a valid region selection on a detail page, focus moves to #comment-input automatically.
- Invalid/tiny selections that are discarded do not move focus.
- Existing keyboard shortcut behavior for / focusing comments remains unchanged.
close_reason: commitDrawState now calls focusCommentInput() after a region is committed.

title: vizdiff: handoff manifest contract for non-interactive callers
description:
vizdiff today is human-driven: a person opens the gallery, picks differences, files issues. That model breaks down when the caller is an automated workflow (CI pre-merge gate, swarm teammate that finished UI work) that needs to hand the visual review off to a human asynchronously.

The capture step is already scriptable — vizline + a project's capture-command produce deterministic PNGs at base and head. What's missing is a stable manifest contract that describes "here is the visual diff that needs human review" in a way a non-interactive caller can produce and a human (or another agent) can consume to launch the gallery later.

Add a documented manifest format and a vizdiff invocation that consumes it:

1. Manifest schema (e.g., manifest.json in an output dir):
   - branch name, base ref SHA, head SHA
   - paired entries: surface name, BASE PNG path, HEAD PNG path, optional one-line "expected change" summary written by the caller
   - optional repo-relative pointer back to the changeset (PR URL, commit list, beads/tracker ID)

2. vizdiff invocation accepting the manifest:
   - bugshot vizdiff --manifest <path> opens the gallery preloaded with the paired surfaces and per-surface summaries.
   - Existing interactive flow (no manifest) continues to work unchanged.

3. Document this as the "non-interactive caller contract" so any tool — CI hook, agent workflow, integrator script — can produce manifests without coupling to a specific orchestrator.

Why the one-line summary matters: a human reviewer opening vizdiff on a branch they didn't write needs to know what they're looking for. Without a summary, gallery review devolves into spot-the-difference. The summary is supplied by whoever produced the manifest (typically the agent/teammate that did the work).

This is independent of bento. A bugshot user without bento can produce manifests from any workflow; a bento user without bugshot is unaffected.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Manifest JSON schema is documented and stable.
2. bugshot vizdiff --manifest <path> opens the gallery with paired surfaces and per-surface summaries displayed.
3. Existing interactive vizdiff (no manifest) is unchanged.
4. Docs describe the non-interactive caller contract end-to-end: capture → write manifest → hand off to human → human runs vizdiff --manifest → reviews → replies through their normal channel.
5. No coupling to any specific orchestrator (bento, swarm, CI, etc.) appears in the bugshot docs.

title: Replace markdown 'Region: path (N points)' with selection-number reference
description:
The markdown draft output renders path-type regions as 'Region: path (100 points).' which is meaningless to a human reader and discards the actual drawing. The textual reduction defeats the purpose of having a visual region annotation.

The full region payload already exists in the JSON output for downstream rendering by the consuming agent. The markdown line should either:
- reference 'Selection N' (matching the on-canvas number) so the human reader knows which drawing the comment is about, or
- be removed entirely, leaving the JSON region as the sole conduit

Recommended: keep a one-line 'Selection N' reference for orientation, and let the consuming agent render the actual region onto the image when filing the issue.

Touches: bugshot_workflow.py:_format_region. Add a unit test asserting the new format and asserting no point-count summary leaks into output.

title: Subdued committed selections; bidirectional hover-highlight between canvas and comment list
description:
Committed selections currently render at full opacity, competing visually with the screenshot. Render them subdued by default, then highlight on hover.

Bidirectional hover linkage:
- Hover a selection on the canvas → highlight the matching comment row in the list
- Hover a comment row in the list → highlight the matching selection on the canvas
- Both directions use the selection number from the foundational numbering work

Hit-testing on the canvas must work for rect, ellipse, and path (path is the hardest — likely per-segment proximity check or a stroke-padded point-in-polyline). Cursor over a selectable region should change to a pointer, distinct from the drawing-mode cursors.

title: Number drawn selections; surface IDs in comment list and on canvas
description:
Comment badges currently show '▭ rect' / '✎ path' — these reduce the geometric meaning to a label that doesn't identify which drawing the comment refers to when there are multiple regions on a unit.

Number selections starting at 1 in the order they were committed within a unit. Comment badge becomes 'Selection N' (no shape-type suffix). On the canvas overlay, render a small numbered badge near each committed selection so users can match comments to drawings visually.

This is foundational for the subdued/hover-highlight work and the markdown output rewrite — both depend on stable selection IDs.

title: Combine drawing tools into single segmented control; cursor reflects active mode
description:
The Tools toolbar has three separate buttons (Off / Rect / Freehand). Replace with one unified control — segmented control or icon strip — that behaves as a single tool selector, so all drawing modes feel like one element.

Mouse cursor over the asset should change based on the active mode:
- off: default
- rect (and ellipse, when added): crosshair
- freehand: pen-style cursor

Touches: templates/detail.html (toolbar markup), static/gallery.js (setActiveTool), static/style.css. Cursor change is currently handled with .tool-active { cursor: crosshair }; needs per-mode classes.

title: Trim bd context injection: .beads/PRIME.md override + memories-only PreCompact + minimal setup block
description:
bd prime injects ~768 words (~1,220 tokens) of mostly static workflow boilerplate at every Claude Code SessionStart, and again at every PreCompact, in any repo with a beads workspace (the beads plugin auto-runs it; some repos also wire it explicitly in .claude/settings.json). bd ships the fix as a supported customization point (see `bd prime --help`): a `.beads/PRIME.md` file overrides the default output entirely; `bd prime --export` dumps the default for editing; `bd prime --memories-only` emits only persistent memories (~45 words).

Work:
1. `bd prime --export > .beads/PRIME.md`, then trim to ~150-200 words keeping only: the condensed session-close checklist (status/add/commit/push), the six core commands (ready, show, create, update --claim, close, dep add), the no-TodoWrite and `bd remember` rules, and a closing pointer ("run `bd prime --full` for the complete reference").
2. Check this repo's `.claude/settings.json` (and be aware of the user-global one) for explicit bd prime hook wiring; where a PreCompact hook runs `bd prime`, switch it to `bd prime --memories-only` — after compaction the summary carries workflow state, and memories are the only content genuinely at risk.
3. Verify before committing: does a PRIME.md override still append persistent memories to SessionStart output? (The help text says the override replaces output "entirely".) If memories are suppressed, either end PRIME.md with an instruction to run `bd memories`, or add `bd prime --memories-only` as a second SessionStart hook.
4. If this repo's AGENTS.md carries a `<!-- generated by bd setup codex -->` ... `<!-- END BEADS CODEX SETUP -->` block, shrink the content inside the markers to two lines: "This repo uses bd (Beads). Run `bd prime` before tracker work. This block is intentionally minimal — do not re-run `bd setup codex`." Only a manual `bd setup codex` re-run regenerates it; `bd doctor` does not rewrite AGENTS.md content.
5. Commit `.beads/PRIME.md` (and any settings/AGENTS.md changes).

Clarifications: step 0 — confirm `bd prime --help` in this repo mentions the PRIME.md override and the `--export`/`--full`/`--memories-only` flags (all verified present in bd as of 2026-06-12; if this repo's bd predates them, upgrade bd or skip with a note). User-global settings (~/.claude/settings.json) are OUT of scope — modify repo-level wiring only and report any user-global bd prime hooks you notice. The provenance reference (bento-jdg) lives in the bento repo's tracker, not this one.

Savings measured in the originating repo: ~1,000 tokens per session start and ~1,150 per compaction. Origin: 2026-06 bento marketplace review (bento-jdg carries the full analysis and the master copy of this workaround).

Acceptance: `bd prime` in this repo emits the trimmed content (word count <= ~220); PreCompact wiring (if explicitly present) uses --memories-only; memories still reach the session (verified per step 3); any setup block is <= ~40 words.

title: Proposal — vizdiff writes a per-screen review-manifest so gates can assert "UI diff was reviewed", not just "artifacts exist"
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

There is a gap between "screenshot artifacts exist" and "a human actually
reviewed them." Bugshot's gallery flow ends when the operator closes the
gallery; nothing machine-checkable records which screens were seen or
commented. A project gate can therefore assert at most that a capture/diff
ran — it must trust prose ("I reviewed the gallery") for the part that
matters.

Evidence is by-extension from the same pickpackit case as the companion
wiring issue bugshot-9u2 (stated explicitly: this is the same incident, not
independent evidence). In /home/ketan/project/pickpackit, a Playwright
demo produced 36 review screenshots per run while a duplicated nav button
and clipped labels sat visible in every screenshot for weeks. Wiring
(bugshot-9u2) explains why vizline/vizdiff never fired; this proposal
addresses the next failure mode up the stack: even with wiring in place,
nothing would let pickpackit's landing gate distinguish "vizdiff ran and
someone looked" from "vizdiff ran and the gallery was closed unseen."

## Desired behavior / change

Proposal: when the operator closes the vizdiff review gallery, vizdiff
writes a per-screen review-manifest recording, for each screen/review
unit, whether it was seen and whether it was commented.

- The machine-checkable marker = manifest existence + completeness (every
  captured screen has a seen entry).
- Partial review fails conservatively: a manifest covering 30 of 36
  screens does NOT satisfy the marker; absence of the manifest does not
  satisfy it either.
- This lets project gates (e.g. a landing checklist or land-work
  precondition) assert "the UI diff for this branch was reviewed" as a
  file check instead of trusting prose.

Design latitude: exact schema, storage location (suggest under
`.bugshot/` beside existing artifacts), and how "seen" is detected in the
gallery UI are implementer decisions; the contract above (existence +
completeness, conservative on partial) is the fixed part.

## Acceptance checks

- Closing the gallery after viewing all screens produces a manifest that
  a one-line check (documented in the README/SKILL.md) accepts.
- Closing the gallery early, or a manifest missing screens, makes that
  same check fail.
- The marker semantics are documented so downstream repos can wire it
  into their own gates without reading bugshot source.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: review-manifest emission from the gallery, the completeness
  check, documentation of the gate contract.
- Out of scope: forcing any particular project to adopt the gate; the
  wiring/installer skill (bugshot-9u2); judging comment quality —
  "commented" is recorded but not required for "seen".

title: vizline SKILL.md contradicts itself about whether it has an enforced trigger
description:
vizline's frontmatter description declares "TRIGGER at branch start before any rendered-output edit when `.agent-plugins/bento/bugshot/viz/capture-command` exists..." — a crisp file-gated trigger. But the skill's Notes section says "vizline ships with no enforced trigger." Both statements are defensible readings (the description is a model-evaluated trigger, not runtime enforcement; the file gate is a condition, not an enforcer) but together they read as self-contradiction and invite agents to discount the trigger. Reword the Notes to state the actual model precisely: the description's file-existence condition determines when the skill SHOULD fire; no runtime hook enforces it; the downstream vizdiff baseline check is the enforcement backstop (stale/missing baseline = hard error).

Acceptance: the two statements are reconciled; an agent reading only the SKILL.md can state when vizline fires and what happens if it is skipped.

Clarification: bugshot has no generated-output rebuild step analogous to bento's; check the repo's own release/versioning convention (plugin.json version) and bump per its practice if one exists.

title: Qualify repo-root-relative spec references in SKILL.md files with the plugin root
description:
`skills/bugshot/SKILL.md:30-31`, `skills/vizline/SKILL.md:44-45`, and `skills/vizdiff/SKILL.md:42-43` reference docs like `docs/specs/2026-04-24-review-units.md` as bare relative paths. The specs ship at the plugin root (verified in the installed cache), but resolved against the agent's cwd — the user's target repo — the paths point at nonexistent files. The skills already establish a `{{bugshot_dir}}` install-dir convention for executables; extend it to the spec references ("`<plugin root>/docs/specs/...`").

Acceptance: every doc reference in the three SKILL.md files resolves from a consumer repo's cwd via the documented convention.

title: Port gallery frontend from JavaScript to TypeScript
description:
Convert static/gallery.js (~2300 lines, single IIFE) to TypeScript. Catch shape errors at build time — region/comment objects, regionState, replaceTarget, the BUGSHOT_DETAIL window payload, etc. all flow as untyped objects today.

Constraints:
- Project's Quick Start emphasizes 'zero external runtime dependencies — stdlib only.' TypeScript would be a build-time dependency only; the served output must remain plain JS bundled into static/.
- Coordinate with bugshot-zza (JS test infrastructure) so the test stack and TS toolchain share a build pipeline.
- Update scripts/build-plugin to compile TS → JS as part of the plugin distribution build.
- Decide whether to keep a single bundled output or split per page (index vs detail).

title: Add unit test for _format_region's unknown-type fallthrough
description: bugshot_workflow._format_region has a final 'return str(region)' branch for unknown region types. tests/test_workflow.py covers rect and path but not the fallthrough. Add a test that passes a region with an unrecognized type and asserts the markdown output contains the str(region) form. Source: docs/plans/2026-04-25-bugshot-region-delta-rebase-handoff.md (Outstanding quality concerns).
close_reason: Obsolete: _format_region was rewritten in commit 103f0dc (bugshot-5wi) and no longer has a str(region) fallthrough. Current branches (selection_id matched / None) are already covered by existing tests in tests/test_workflow.py.

title: Scope setActiveTool's .btn-tool query to #detail-tools
description: static/gallery.js setActiveTool calls document.querySelectorAll('.btn-tool'), which would pick up any .btn-tool button anywhere on the page. Scope the query to #detail-tools to be defensive against future markup that reuses the class. Source: docs/plans/2026-04-25-bugshot-region-delta-rebase-handoff.md (Outstanding quality concerns).

title: Extract paintRegion lineWidth into a named constant
description: static/gallery.js paintRegion uses a hardcoded lineWidth = 2. Extract to a named module-scope constant alongside REGION_LINE_COLOR, REGION_FILL_COLOR, etc., for consistency with the other region-drawing constants. Source: docs/plans/2026-04-25-bugshot-region-delta-rebase-handoff.md (Outstanding quality concerns).
close_reason: Already implemented by commit 162a2aa (bugshot-qh9). static/gallery.js:51-52 defines REGION_DEFAULT_LINE_WIDTH=2 and REGION_HIGHLIGHT_LINE_WIDTH=3 at module scope; paintRegion uses them at lines 1520-1522. Naming matches the REGION_* family.

title: Detail-page legend statically advertises 'd cycle tool' even when region drawing is gated off
description: On the detail page, templates/detail.html includes 'd cycle tool' in the shortcut legend for every unit. Region drawing is gated by unitSupportsRegionDrawing(currentUnit), so on multi-asset / ANSI / non-image units the 'd' keypress is a clean no-op — but the legend overpromises. Fix: render the legend in JS conditional on unit shape, or split it server-side per unit type. Source: docs/plans/2026-04-25-bugshot-region-delta-rebase-handoff.md (Outstanding quality concerns).

title: Arrow-key navigation through comments; activate associated selection
description:
Add keyboard navigation between comment rows using ArrowUp/ArrowDown (or j/k). When focus moves to a comment whose region carries a selection_id, highlight the matching selection on the canvas (same highlight state as hover from bugshot-qh9). When focus is on a comment whose region is null (image-level), clear any active canvas highlight.

Depends on bugshot-qh9 landing first because it introduces the highlight state and bidirectional wiring this builds on.

Acceptance:
- ArrowUp/ArrowDown moves focus between .comment-item rows in the comment list
- The focused row gets the same .is-hovered visual treatment as on mouseenter
- If the focused row has data-selection-id, the canvas paints that selection in the highlighted style; otherwise the canvas reverts to all-subdued
- Wraps at top/bottom (ArrowUp on first row goes to last, ArrowDown on last row goes to first) — or doesn't, choose based on what feels right; document the choice
- Works only when no input/textarea is focused

title: Add ellipse drawing mode to region tools
description:
Currently only rectangle and freehand are supported. Add an ellipse drawing mode (free aspect ratio) for marking circular/oval regions.

Region payload: {type: 'ellipse', cx, cy, rx, ry} with all coordinates normalized to [0, 1].

Implementation:
- Add TOOL_ELLIPSE constant + button in the unified tools control
- mousedown/mousemove/mouseup updates a pending ellipse anchored at the down-point
- redraw() paints ellipses with the same dashed-pending / solid-committed convention used for rect
- Hit-testing for the hover-highlight feature must understand ellipses (separate issue)
- Update skills/bugshot/SKILL.md draft schema with the ellipse shape
- Add unit tests for region serialization round-trip

title: Refine detail-page shortcut legend (drop 'unit id' in flat mode; improve readability)
description:
In flat-screenshot mode the unit id IS the filename, so 'c copy filename / unit id' in the legend is redundant — drop the '/ unit id' portion when the review root is flat. Legend has also grown long enough to be hard to scan; consider a two-row layout or a fixed-width font for predictable alignment.

Touches: templates/detail.html (likely move to JS-rendered legend so flat-mode can be conditional), static/gallery.js, static/style.css.
